articles.title: Where to watch Delta Goodrem's Eurovision final performance

children.content: The Eurovision Song Contest is on this Sunday, which will mean an early start for superfans of the pop culture phenomenon.

image.altText: A woman sitting at a piano and singing.

children.content: Delta Goodrem will perform eighth in the Eurovision grand final, where she is one of the favourites to win. Follow live.

items.title: Will drastic NDIS overhaul repeat the mistakes of the past?

children.content: The states are once again on the hook for disability services due to the Commonwealth's drastic NDIS cuts. Can the mistakes of the past be avoided?

image.altText: A middle-aged man looking out to a gated, prison-like area

items.title: Aspiration has changed since the Howard era. This budget is finally catching up

children.content: At the turn of the century, "aspiration" described Australians who wanted to put their kids into private school and university, and to build their wealth by getting an investment property or two. Today, it means something humbler; millions of younger Australians simply aspire to own a home one day, to have some shelter.

image.altText: John Howard and Peter Costello hold a media conference.

items.title: Three controversies sparked by Trump's reflecting pool paint job

children.content: Donald Trump has chosen a new colour for Washington DC's Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. Here are three reasons why it's proving to be controversial.

image.altText: The Lincoln Memorial is seen behind an empty reflecting pool, which is partially painted blue.

items.title: 'You're malnourished; you are overtrained': Olympic gymnast's warning

children.content: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva trained up to 10 hours a day, six days a week, often without the proper nutrition. She wants others to know what that can do to the body.

image.altText: A gymnast does the splits while jumping and holding a ball.

items.title: Rodrigues thought AI would make him rich. Then everything spiralled

children.content: Rodrigues thought an AI chatbot could help him escape poverty and build wealth. Instead, he became trapped in a spiral of delusion and dependence.

items.title: Why some people pay more to avoid convential home loans

children.content: When Wildan decided to buy a property he didn't even consider a conventional loan. Instead, he opted for a niche option that caters to a growing segment of Australia's population.

items.title: Top Hamas military leader killed in Israeli air strikes, official confirms

children.content: Izz al-Din al-Haddad is the most senior Hamas official to be killed by Israel since a US-backed ceasefire agreement meant to halt fighting was implemented in October.

items.title: Labor on track to win by-election in Brisbane seat of Stafford

children.content: At the end of counting on Saturday night, there has been a 4.1 per cent swing against Labor after preferences, with additional postal votes still to be counted.

items.title: Friends witness 'horrific' shark attack that claimed life of spearfishing mate

children.content: A 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with friends when he was killed in a shark attack off Rottnest Island on Saturday morning.

items.title: Eight dead and 35 injured after train collides with public bus in Bangkok

children.content: Footage of the incident shows a group of vehicles queuing across a railway line, before a cargo train suddenly ploughs into the side of the bus and pushes it into oncoming traffic.

image.altText: A still from CCTV footage shows a train pushing a flaming bus through a crowd of cars.

items.title: 'Don't be a d**k, Dezi!': New claims about Freeman police shooting

items.title: Who was still watching Harvey Weinstein almost a decade on from #MeToo?

items.title: Taylor vows to alter Sex Discrimination Act following 'Tickle v Giggle' ruling

items.title: Artist appointed, axed then reinstated from Venice Biennale triumphs

children.content: Last year, Khaled Sabsabi was appointed, sacked and then reinstated as Australia's representative to the Venice Biennale. Now he opens two installations at the world's most prestigious art exhibition.

items.title: Disabled Australians on love, lust and the right to intimacy

items.title: After her husband's death, Annie discovered he had placed 100 bets a day

children.content: Annie Ashton's husband took his own life after suffering from a gambling addiction. Since then, she's campaigned for stronger regulation of the gambling industry and greater awareness of gambling harm.

items.title: Origin hopefuls stand up as Cowboys coach hails 'gritty' win

items.title: A Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something much bigger

items.title: Is the US-China rivalry over?

children.content: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.

items.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

items.title: Stolen car involved in alleged shooting spotted at SA prison staff car park days earlier
